name: authentication-logic
description: Guide to using Better Auth for client and server-side authentication.
qualityReason: Specific to Better Auth and Next.js
capabilities: Configure Better Auth client and server instances
Implement email and social sign-in with Better Auth
Protect API routes using session validation
Integrate AuthBar component for user avatars and login buttons
Handle session state in client components
author: AbdulSamad94
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill. All 25 static findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified markdown code block delimiters (backticks) as shell execution, security recommendations as vulnerabilities, and metadata fields as credential access. No actual executable code, network calls, file system operations, or credential handling exists in this skill. The content is purely educational documentation for the Better Auth library.
valueStatement: Building secure user authentication requires understanding both client and server-side patterns. This skill provides a practical guide to implementing authentication using Better Auth in Next.js applications with clear code examples.
limitations: Does not include authentication middleware configuration
Does not cover database adapter setup
Does not provide custom authentication provider implementation
Does not include OAuth provider configuration details

useCases.title: Add auth to Next.js apps
useCases.description: Implement secure user authentication in Next.js applications using Better Auth patterns.

useCases.title: Build auth UI components
useCases.description: Create login forms, sign-out buttons, and user avatar displays in client components.

useCases.title: Protect API endpoints
useCases.description: Secure server-side routes and actions by validating user sessions.

promptTemplates.title: Basic sign-in setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up email sign-in using Better Auth in a Next.js client component?

promptTemplates.title: Social login
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement Google social sign-in with Better Auth and handle the callback.

promptTemplates.title: Server protection
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I protect a Next.js API route using auth.api.getSession?

promptTemplates.title: Auth UI integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an AuthBar component that shows user avatar when signed in and login button when signed out.

bestPractices: Always validate sessions on the server side before granting access to protected resources
Use environment variables for sensitive authentication configuration
Implement proper error handling for authentication failures
antiPatterns: Storing authentication tokens in localStorage without proper encryption
Skipping server-side session validation for protected routes
Hardcoding OAuth credentials directly in source code

faq.question: What databases does Better Auth support?
faq.answer: Better Auth supports multiple adapters including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, SQLite, and Drizzle ORM.

faq.question: Can I use Better Auth with React Server Components?
faq.answer: Yes, Better Auth provides server-side APIs that work seamlessly with React Server Components and Server Actions.

faq.question: How long do Better Auth sessions last?
faq.answer: Session duration is configurable. Default sessions persist until expiration or user signs out.

faq.question: Is user data stored securely?
faq.answer: Better Auth encrypts sensitive data and works with your database adapter's security features.

faq.question: Why is my session returning null?
faq.answer: Check that cookies are being passed correctly using headers() and that the session has not expired.

faq.question: How does Better Auth compare to NextAuth.js?
faq.answer: Better Auth offers simpler configuration, better TypeScript support, and more flexible plugin architecture.
